title: Kendrick Lamar Makes Grammys History as Most-Awarded Rapper, Breaking Jay-Z's Record
text: At the 68th Grammy Awards on February 1, 2026, Kendrick Lamar surpassed Jay-Z to become hip-hop's most decorated artist at the ceremony. The rapper claimed four awards during the televised event, bringing his total Grammy count to 26. Lamar's victories included best rap album for GNX, best rap song for TV Off, melodic rap performance for Luther, and rap performance for his feature on Clipse's Chains & Whips. With 26 Grammys, Lamar now holds the record previously held by Jay-Z, who has 25 awards. Kanye West ranks third with 24 trophies. The 9-time nominee still had opportunities to increase his total, with major categories like album of the year and song of the year remaining. Upon receiving the best rap album award, Lamar stated: It is hip-hop as usual, man. I'm not good at talking about myself, but I express it through the music. Lamar's historic win caps a remarkable trajectory that includes three previous best rap album victories and widespread recognition as one of hip-hop's most influential contemporary artists.
